marketName: Museums, Historical Sites, and Similar Institutions
coreJobStatement: Acquire, preserve, and present cultural, historical, natural, and scientific heritage to the public through curated exhibitions, conservation, and educational programming
productJobStatement: Help Building Security & Life Safety to detect and deter unauthorized use of emergency exit doors in collection spaces and storage areas while maintaining fire-code-compliant escape capability in heritage buildings where structural modification is prohibited or restricted
lPath: L6 Building Security & Life Safety > L5 Emergency Exit Security Devices

segments.name: Large art / natural history museum
segments.characteristics: >200K visitors/yr; professional security team; high-value portable collection; BMS likely present
segments.targetabilityReason: Primary theft prevention concern; highest consequence per undetected exit event; IoT dashboard replaces costly guard post
segments.circumstance: >200K visitors/yr; professional security team; high-value portable collection; BMS likely present
segments.alternativesDiffer: IoT dashboard replaces costly guard post; primary theft prevention concern; highest consequence per undetected exit event

segments.name: Historic house / heritage site (listed building)
segments.characteristics: Protected structure; cannot route wiring without heritage consent; minimal permanent staff
segments.targetabilityReason: C5 (surface mount) is an advantage, not a friction; battery-powered wireless is the only viable retrofit option
segments.circumstance: Protected structure; cannot route wiring without heritage consent; minimal permanent staff
segments.alternativesDiffer: Wired A2 alternative is disqualified without planning consent; battery-powered wireless is the only viable retrofit option

segments.name: Regional / community museum
segments.characteristics: Small paid staff + volunteers; limited technical capacity; tight budget
segments.targetabilityReason: IoT price premium (C7) is a barrier; ROI must be simple and documentable; commissioning complexity is a decision killer
segments.circumstance: Small paid staff + volunteers; limited technical capacity; tight budget
segments.alternativesDiffer: IoT price premium is a barrier; mechanical-only or do-nothing dominates

segments.name: Archaeological / outdoor site
segments.characteristics: Dispersed perimeter; intermittent public access seasons; remote or semi-remote locations
segments.targetabilityReason: Range constraint (C3) is primary; battery life (C4) critical where maintenance visits are infrequent; no BMS
segments.circumstance: Dispersed perimeter; intermittent public access seasons; remote or semi-remote locations
segments.alternativesDiffer: Range constraint and battery life are primary risks; product fit is weaker at remote outdoor sites

segments.name: Multi-site museum group / national body
segments.characteristics: Manages portfolio of buildings under one security governance structure
segments.targetabilityReason: Cross-site IoT dashboard is the decisive value proposition; protocol lock-in (C6) relevant if centralized BMS is in scope
segments.circumstance: Manages portfolio of buildings under one security governance structure
segments.alternativesDiffer: Cross-site IoT dashboard is the decisive value proposition; no single-site solution aggregates

alternatives.name: Basic mechanical door alarm (no monitoring) — 45% share
alternatives.tradeoffs: No remote visibility; requires physical patrol to detect unreported activation; no audit log; no battery required for alarm

alternatives.name: Wired intruder detection + door contacts (alarm panel) — 25% share
alternatives.tradeoffs: Wiring through heritage fabric requires planning consent and conservation officer approval — major barrier in listed buildings; high retrofit cost

alternatives.name: Alarmed exit hardware with IoT monitoring (Turkwachter) — 20% share
alternatives.tradeoffs: Proprietary wireless protocol (C6); battery dependency (C4); ~730 EUR/door (C7); surface-mount profile is accepted in heritage context

alternatives.name: Gallery attendant / security guard patrol
alternatives.tradeoffs: High recurring labor cost (security = ~22% of museum operating budget); coverage gaps when attendant is elsewhere; no event log; scales linearly with door count

alternatives.name: No dedicated exit monitoring (do nothing) — 10% share
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero installation burden; zero P1 burden; compliance risk distributed across insurance, fire marshal, and grant-funding conditions

jobSteps.description: Determine which emergency exits require monitoring (storage vaults, conservation labs, galleries, public exits), alert priority by zone, and notification escalation rules
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Zone configuration is critical; vault exits > public gallery exits
jobSteps.rawStatement: Determine which emergency exits require monitoring, alert priority by zone, and notification escalation rules
jobSteps.rationale: Zone configuration by collection risk level; vault exits require higher-priority alert rules than public gallery exits

jobSteps.description: Map all emergency exit points across building; assess wireless signal paths through historic masonry; identify gateway placement compatible with heritage constraints
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: RF penetration through thick stone/brick walls is a key variable
jobSteps.rawStatement: Map all emergency exit points across building; assess wireless signal paths through historic masonry
jobSteps.rationale: RF coverage planning through heritage-grade masonry; gateway placement subject to conservation constraints

jobSteps.description: Install monitoring devices on door handles without drilling into historic fabric; pair to gateway; configure zone priorities; test alarm tone audibility
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Surface-mount, no-drill installation is the primary market differentiator
jobSteps.rawStatement: Install monitoring devices on door handles without drilling into historic fabric
jobSteps.rationale: Surface-mount installation without drilling; heritage officer sign-off on reversibility

jobSteps.description: Verify every device transmits through heritage-grade masonry walls, alarm is unobstructed, escape motion is unrestricted, and heritage officer confirms reversibility
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Acceptance testing must include RF validation through actual wall materials
jobSteps.rawStatement: Verify every device transmits correctly through actual building materials and alarm operation is unimpeded
jobSteps.rationale: Acceptance test of radio transmission through actual wall materials; alarm audibility and escape motion verification

jobSteps.description: Maintain all monitored exits in secured state 24/7; trigger alert on unauthorized activation; distinguish high-risk zones (storage) from lower-risk zones (public exits)
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Core function; after-hours monitoring is the primary use case
jobSteps.rawStatement: Maintain all monitored exits in secured state; trigger alert on unauthorized activation
jobSteps.rationale: 24/7 exit security with heightened after-hours theft-prevention priority

jobSteps.description: Track real-time exit status from centralized dashboard; receive alerts prioritized by zone risk level; correlate with CCTV on high-risk events
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Primary IoT value; after-hours dashboard access on mobile is essential for lean security teams
jobSteps.rawStatement: Track real-time exit status from centralized dashboard; receive alerts prioritized by zone risk level
jobSteps.rationale: After-hours dashboard -- primary IoT value for lean or remote security teams

jobSteps.description: Respond to and reset triggered devices; adjust zone alert rules; replace failed units with minimal disruption to gallery operations or open days
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Reset must not require tool or key unavailable on night shift
jobSteps.rawStatement: Respond to and reset triggered devices; adjust zone alert rules
jobSteps.rationale: Alert zone rule adjustment; device reset by duty staff without specialist tools

jobSteps.description: Generate compliance audit reports for fire marshal, insurer, and heritage authority; proactively replace batteries before monitoring capability is lost
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Compliance documentation is a grant-funding and insurance requirement for many institutions
jobSteps.rawStatement: Generate compliance audit reports for fire marshal, insurer, and heritage authority
jobSteps.rationale: Compliance documentation for fire marshal, insurer, heritage funder; battery health management in conservation environments

stakeholders.who: Insurer; heritage funder (Arts Council, Historic England); fire authority

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of high-value collection storage area exits remaining unmonitored due to incomplete upfront coverage planning, leaving >=1 after-hours exit event undetected per quarter

Increase the ability to segment emergency exit monitoring alerts by zone risk level (storage vault vs. public gallery vs. service corridor) so that a single after-hours activation of a vault exit triggers immediate escalation
Increase the ability to pre-validate wireless signal coverage through heritage-grade masonry walls (stone, brick, reinforced concrete) before committing to gateway placement and device purchase
Minimize the likelihood of RF signal loss between exit devices and the gateway caused by thick historic masonry walls, creating silent monitoring gaps exceeding 30 minutes
Minimize the time required to install and commission emergency exit monitoring across a 20-room museum without drilling into historic fabric -- achieving full coverage in <1 day
Minimize the likelihood of installation fixing methods leaving permanent marks or penetrations in listed building fabric, causing non-compliance with heritage conservation officer requirements

Increase the ability to verify that every installed exit device transmits correctly through actual building materials and that alarm operation is unimpeded -- within a single commissioning walk-through that the heritage officer can witness
Minimize the likelihood of an unauthorized emergency exit activation in a storage vault or conservation area remaining undetected for >2 minutes during closed hours
Minimize the likelihood of an emergency exit monitoring device adding any resistance or additional step to the escape path that could delay evacuation of visitors with reduced mobility

Minimize the likelihood of legitimate authorized uses (staff exiting at end of shift, conservation team accessing storage) generating false alerts that erode duty manager confidence in the monitoring system

Increase the ability of a single duty security officer or remote monitoring team to track real-time exit status across all monitored doors from one centralized interface without physical patrol -- during closed-hours coverage windows
Minimize the time from emergency exit activation event to security officer notification to <60 seconds during after-hours monitoring periods
Minimize the likelihood of wireless connectivity loss to any monitored exit device creating a silent gap exceeding 15 minutes -- particularly during after-hours periods when no staff are on-site

Minimize the time to reset a triggered exit monitoring device and restore it to active monitoring state after an incident investigation -- without requiring specialist tools not available to on-duty museum staff
Minimize the likelihood of a high-priority storage area activation being deprioritized due to alert fatigue from higher-frequency low-risk public gallery exit events

Increase the ability to produce a complete, timestamped digital log of all emergency exit activations and response actions formatted for submission to the fire authority, insurer, and heritage funder -- without manual compilation

Minimize the time required to identify and proactively replace batteries approaching depletion threshold across a deployment of >=10 exit monitoring devices before monitoring capability is lost -- accounting for reduced battery performance in climate-controlled conservation environments

Minimize the variability in emergency exit monitoring coverage status across the building at any time -- ensuring no exits enter an unmonitored state due to technical failure without triggering a staff notification

Minimize the likelihood of emergency exit compliance deficiencies being cited during fire marshal inspection or heritage authority review -- resulting in temporary closure of gallery spaces or loss of grant-funding eligibility
Increase the ability to demonstrate evidence-based, timestamped response to after-hours exit activations -- to insurers and collections trustees -- without manual log reconstruction
Minimize the likelihood of a preventable exit monitoring failure becoming a documented finding in an insurer audit or heritage authority inspection that affects funding eligibility
Minimize the sense of uncertainty about whether high-risk storage and collection exits are truly secured during closed-hours periods when no patrol is possible
